uploadedResumes.extractedText:
SRIGANTH VARATHARAJ ABOUT ME  A   Computer Science and Engineering student with a strong interest in software  development, backed by hands-on experience in Java and object-oriented design. Familiar with frontend technologies through a virtual internship, where I worked on building user interfaces, resolving bugs, and organizing reusable components. Actively seeking a Software Engineer Trainee role to apply and grow my technical skills in a real-world environment.  6369434256   sriganthvaratharaj@gmail.com   Namakkal,Tamilnadu  EDUCATION  HSC   – Reliance Matric Higher Secondary School, Komarapalayam - 90.3%   (2022)  SSLC   – Government Boys Higher Secondary School, Komarapalayam - 89.4%   (2020)  Bachelor of Computer Science and Engineering   (CGPA : 7.8)  Sri Shanmugha College of Engineering and Technology   (2022 – 2026)  CERTIFICATES  Introduction to HTML5 – University of Michigan (Coursera)  UI/UX   -   Skill Dragon WORK EXPERIENCE  Frontend Web Development Intern — MotionCut  Virtual Internship | Oct 2024 – Nov 2024  Completed a structured one-month internship focused on practical frontend  development tasks and UI implementation.  Built three mini frontend projects applying responsive layout techniques,  improving visual consistency and usability.  HackerRank   LeetCode GitHub LinkedIn  TECHNICAL SKILLS  Core Java  HTML5, CSS, Bootstrap  Design Tools: Canva, Figma  Currently Learning: Java (Springboot)

experience.description: Virtual Internship | Completed a structured one-month internship focused on practical frontend development tasks and UI implementation. Built three mini frontend projects applying responsive layout techniques, improving visual consistency and usability.

improvements: Reformat the 'TECHNICAL SKILLS' section with clear bullet points or categories instead of a single paragraph to improve ATS parsing.

Add quantified results to the 'WORK EXPERIENCE' bullets. For example, specify the impact of the 'responsive layout techniques' (e.g., 'improved page load time by X%' or 'increased user engagement metrics').
Create a distinct 'Skills' section with categorized lists (e.g., 'Programming Languages:', 'Web Technologies:', 'Tools:') for better clarity and keyword scanning.
The 'CERTIFICATES' section is incomplete; the 'UI/UX - Skill Dragon' entry lacks a clear title or completion date. Standardize this formatting.
Consider renaming 'About Me' to 'Summary' or 'Objective' for a more professional and standard header.
strengths: Standard section headers are used (About Me, Education, etc.), which is ATS-friendly.
Relevant technical keywords for a software role are present (Java, HTML5, CSS).
The 'About Me' section effectively serves as a summary/objective.
normalATS.summary: This resume is from a student/fresher candidate and has a clear, simple structure with standard sections. The main weaknesses are a lack of quantified achievements, a missing dedicated 'Skills' section, and formatting inconsistencies that could hinder ATS parsing.

education.degree: Bachelor of Computer Science and Engineering
education.institution: Sri Shanmugha College of Engineering and Technology

improvements: Since the JD requires Docker, add a Docker project to your GitHub portfolio demonstrating containerization of a simple web application
For the Node.js requirement, build and showcase a Node.js project on GitHub - consider creating a simple REST API or integrating Node.js with your frontend skills
Add your MotionCut internship details more prominently with specific UI/UX accomplishments and technologies used
strengths: Completed structured frontend internship with practical project experience
Active learner currently expanding Java/Spring Boot skills
Has UI/UX certification showing design foundation
atsAnalysis.summary: Computer Science student with frontend development internship experience and basic UI/UX skills. While the resume shows relevant frontend capabilities for a UI Designer role, it lacks the specific Docker and Node.js technologies mentioned in the job description. The candidate is actively learning Spring Boot showing good initiative.
